agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly conflicting, non-negotiable requirements about whether courier GPS location should be visible to customers.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Customer Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver assigned to transport a package from origin to customer destination"},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The courier's GPS coordinates updated continuously during an active delivery"},{"term":"Active delivery","definition":"The period from when a courier picks up a package until it is delivered to the customer"},{"term":"Customer visibility","definition":"The degree to which delivery tracking information is exposed to the end customer"},{"term":"Courier privacy","definition":"The protection of a courier's precise movements and location data from external parties"}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"During an active delivery, the system must maintain the courier's GPS location internally for dispatch and operational purposes.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Without internal location tracking, dispatch, routing, and ETA calculations cannot function. This is a core operational invariant regardless of how the conflict is resolved.","testable_assertion":"Given a courier on an active delivery, the system always has a current GPS fix for that courier available to internal operations systems."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Customer-facing delivery status must provide some form of progress indication during active delivery.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The form and granularity of progress indication is a business policy decision, not a system integrity constraint. Could range from exact GPS to simple stage-based updates.","testable_assertion":"Given an active delivery, the customer can see at least one form of delivery progress (e.g., status stage, ETA, or location)."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Courier personal data and precise location must not be exposed in a way that creates safety or retention risk for couriers.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Exposing courier location in a way that enables surveillance, stalking, or harassment is a physical safety constraint. If violated, the platform is endangering its workforce.","testable_assertion":"Given any customer-facing tracking view, a third party cannot derive the courier's precise real-time coordinates to within street-address accuracy when the courier is not at the delivery destination."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Customers must receive transparency into delivery progress sufficient to build trust and reduce 'where is my order' support contacts.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The level of transparency is a business policy choice balancing customer experience against operational constraints. A VP could adjust the granularity.","testable_assertion":"Given an active delivery, the customer can determine approximate delivery progress and expected arrival without contacting support."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A delivery is in 'picked up' or 'in transit' status","when":"The customer views the tracking page","then":"The system displays delivery progress information (the exact form is blocked pending resolution of the stakeholder conflict)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A courier is on an active delivery","when":"Internal dispatch queries the courier's location","then":"The system returns the courier's current GPS coordinates with sub-60-second freshness"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"The stakeholder conflict is resolved with any chosen approach","when":"The feature is live for 30 days","then":"Courier retention rate has not degraded compared to the pre-feature baseline"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"The stakeholder conflict is resolved with any chosen approach","when":"The feature is live for 30 days","then":"Customer 'where is my order' support ticket volume has decreased or held steady compared to the pre-feature baseline"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for an extended period (e.g., traffic, break) and exact GPS is shown","expected_behavior":"If exact GPS is chosen, a stationary pin reveals the courier's precise idle location, amplifying the surveillance concern raised by Operations"},{"scenario":"Customer uses real-time GPS to intercept or confront a courier mid-route","expected_behavior":"This is the physical safety risk Operations is flagging — precise location enables in-person confrontation before the courier reaches the destination"},{"scenario":"Courier is near the delivery address but GPS shows them on a different street","expected_behavior":"GPS inaccuracy near the destination could generate false 'courier is lost' complaints, increasing support load rather than reducing it"},{"scenario":"Delivery marked as active but courier has not moved for 30+ minutes","expected_behavior":"Showing exact GPS of a motionless courier raises customer anxiety and complaint escalation; showing approximate progress or ETA avoids this"},{"scenario":"Customer screenshots GPS trail and posts courier's route on social media","expected_behavior":"Precise GPS creates a data leak vector for courier movement patterns, which is both a privacy and a safety issue"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"High risk in both directions. Losing couriers (Operations concern) directly increases recruitment and training costs — typically $3K-5K per courier in the gig economy. Losing customers due to poor transparency (Customer Success concern) reduces order volume and lifetime value. The wrong resolution is expensive either way.","operational":"Courier retention is an existential operational constraint — without drivers, deliveries cannot happen regardless of customer satisfaction. However, excessive support contacts from opaque tracking also strain operations. Resolution must balance both.","user":"Customers want confidence their delivery is progressing. Couriers want to feel like professionals, not surveilled gig workers. Both are 'users' of the platform and both expectations are legitimate.","risk":"Deploying exact GPS without resolving this conflict risks immediate courier attrition. Deploying no tracking risks customer churn and increased support load. Either unilateral decision creates measurable business damage.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Desire real-time visibility into delivery progress to reduce anxiety and build trust in the platform"},{"group":"Couriers","impact":"Report feeling surveilled by exact GPS sharing; this is causing measurable attrition and is framed as a safety and dignity concern"},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Accountable for customer satisfaction metrics; sees transparency as a competitive differentiator"},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Accountable for courier supply and retention; has data showing driver loss attributed to location sharing"},{"group":"Executive leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict — it is a strategic prioritization decision that cannot be resolved at the team level"}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The spec contains an explicit, irreconcilable contradiction between two non-negotiable stakeholder requirements. Both teams have legitimate business justifications. No amount of domain analysis can resolve a political conflict — this requires executive arbitration. Confidence is low because the core feature requirement (what to show the customer) is undefined until this is resolved.","high_confidence_areas":["Internal courier GPS tracking is required regardless of resolution","Both stakeholder concerns are legitimate and well-grounded in real business risk","The system must track courier location internally for dispatch and ETA","Some form of customer-facing progress indication is needed"],"low_confidence_areas":["The exact granularity of customer-facing location data cannot be determined without stakeholder resolution","Whether approximate location (e.g., zone-based, ETA-only) would satisfy Customer Success","Whether any form of location sharing would satisfy Operations' courier retention concern","Industry benchmarks on courier attrition vs. tracking granularity"],"assumptions":["Both teams are accurately representing their constraints — couriers are actually leaving, and customers actually want this","There is no existing executive directive that already resolves this conflict","The platform has the technical capability to offer variable granularity (exact GPS, zone-based, ETA-only)"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates visible to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires courier location to never be shown to customers. These are mutually exclusive, non-negotiable requirements from peer-level teams. Neither team has the authority to override the other.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers","Customer Success team","Operations team","Executive leadership"],"options":["A: Show exact GPS (satisfies Customer Success, violates Operations requirement, risks courier attrition)","B: Show no location (satisfies Operations, violates Customer Success requirement, risks customer churn)","C: Compromise — show approximate zone or ETA-based tracking instead of exact GPS (may partially satisfy both, requires both teams to soften their 'non-negotiable' stance)","D: Make it courier-opt-in — couriers choose whether to share location (respects courier autonomy but creates inconsistent customer experience)"],"recommendation":"Escalate to executive leadership for arbitration. Recommend Option C (approximate/zone-based tracking) as the starting point for discussion — it addresses the customer transparency need without enabling precise surveillance. However, this is a business strategy decision, not a domain analysis decision. Do not implement anything until this is resolved."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot implement exact GPS visibility as specified by Customer Success","business_rule_broken":"BR-3 — Courier safety and retention constraint. Showing precise real-time coordinates enables surveillance behavior that Operations has documented as causing driver attrition."},{"violation":"Cannot implement zero customer visibility as specified by Operations","business_rule_broken":"BR-4 — Customer transparency requirement. Providing no delivery progress information undermines the core value proposition of the tracking feature and increases support load."}]}

content.text: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly conflicting, non-negotiable requirements about whether courier GPS location should be visible to customers.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Customer Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver assigned to transport a package from origin to customer destination"},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The courier's GPS coordinates updated continuously during an active delivery"},{"term":"Active delivery","definition":"The period from when a courier picks up a package until it is delivered to the customer"},{"term":"Customer visibility","definition":"The degree to which delivery tracking information is exposed to the end customer"},{"term":"Courier privacy","definition":"The protection of a courier's precise movements and location data from external parties"}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"During an active delivery, the system must maintain the courier's GPS location internally for dispatch and operational purposes.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Without internal location tracking, dispatch, routing, and ETA calculations cannot function. This is a core operational invariant regardless of how the conflict is resolved.","testable_assertion":"Given a courier on an active delivery, the system always has a current GPS fix for that courier available to internal operations systems."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Customer-facing delivery status must provide some form of progress indication during active delivery.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The form and granularity of progress indication is a business policy decision, not a system integrity constraint. Could range from exact GPS to simple stage-based updates.","testable_assertion":"Given an active delivery, the customer can see at least one form of delivery progress (e.g., status stage, ETA, or location)."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Courier personal data and precise location must not be exposed in a way that creates safety or retention risk for couriers.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Exposing courier location in a way that enables surveillance, stalking, or harassment is a physical safety constraint. If violated, the platform is endangering its workforce.","testable_assertion":"Given any customer-facing tracking view, a third party cannot derive the courier's precise real-time coordinates to within street-address accuracy when the courier is not at the delivery destination."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Customers must receive transparency into delivery progress sufficient to build trust and reduce 'where is my order' support contacts.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The level of transparency is a business policy choice balancing customer experience against operational constraints. A VP could adjust the granularity.","testable_assertion":"Given an active delivery, the customer can determine approximate delivery progress and expected arrival without contacting support."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A delivery is in 'picked up' or 'in transit' status","when":"The customer views the tracking page","then":"The system displays delivery progress information (the exact form is blocked pending resolution of the stakeholder conflict)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A courier is on an active delivery","when":"Internal dispatch queries the courier's location","then":"The system returns the courier's current GPS coordinates with sub-60-second freshness"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"The stakeholder conflict is resolved with any chosen approach","when":"The feature is live for 30 days","then":"Courier retention rate has not degraded compared to the pre-feature baseline"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"The stakeholder conflict is resolved with any chosen approach","when":"The feature is live for 30 days","then":"Customer 'where is my order' support ticket volume has decreased or held steady compared to the pre-feature baseline"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for an extended period (e.g., traffic, break) and exact GPS is shown","expected_behavior":"If exact GPS is chosen, a stationary pin reveals the courier's precise idle location, amplifying the surveillance concern raised by Operations"},{"scenario":"Customer uses real-time GPS to intercept or confront a courier mid-route","expected_behavior":"This is the physical safety risk Operations is flagging — precise location enables in-person confrontation before the courier reaches the destination"},{"scenario":"Courier is near the delivery address but GPS shows them on a different street","expected_behavior":"GPS inaccuracy near the destination could generate false 'courier is lost' complaints, increasing support load rather than reducing it"},{"scenario":"Delivery marked as active but courier has not moved for 30+ minutes","expected_behavior":"Showing exact GPS of a motionless courier raises customer anxiety and complaint escalation; showing approximate progress or ETA avoids this"},{"scenario":"Customer screenshots GPS trail and posts courier's route on social media","expected_behavior":"Precise GPS creates a data leak vector for courier movement patterns, which is both a privacy and a safety issue"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"High risk in both directions. Losing couriers (Operations concern) directly increases recruitment and training costs — typically $3K-5K per courier in the gig economy. Losing customers due to poor transparency (Customer Success concern) reduces order volume and lifetime value. The wrong resolution is expensive either way.","operational":"Courier retention is an existential operational constraint — without drivers, deliveries cannot happen regardless of customer satisfaction. However, excessive support contacts from opaque tracking also strain operations. Resolution must balance both.","user":"Customers want confidence their delivery is progressing. Couriers want to feel like professionals, not surveilled gig workers. Both are 'users' of the platform and both expectations are legitimate.","risk":"Deploying exact GPS without resolving this conflict risks immediate courier attrition. Deploying no tracking risks customer churn and increased support load. Either unilateral decision creates measurable business damage.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Desire real-time visibility into delivery progress to reduce anxiety and build trust in the platform"},{"group":"Couriers","impact":"Report feeling surveilled by exact GPS sharing; this is causing measurable attrition and is framed as a safety and dignity concern"},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Accountable for customer satisfaction metrics; sees transparency as a competitive differentiator"},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Accountable for courier supply and retention; has data showing driver loss attributed to location sharing"},{"group":"Executive leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict — it is a strategic prioritization decision that cannot be resolved at the team level"}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The spec contains an explicit, irreconcilable contradiction between two non-negotiable stakeholder requirements. Both teams have legitimate business justifications. No amount of domain analysis can resolve a political conflict — this requires executive arbitration. Confidence is low because the core feature requirement (what to show the customer) is undefined until this is resolved.","high_confidence_areas":["Internal courier GPS tracking is required regardless of resolution","Both stakeholder concerns are legitimate and well-grounded in real business risk","The system must track courier location internally for dispatch and ETA","Some form of customer-facing progress indication is needed"],"low_confidence_areas":["The exact granularity of customer-facing location data cannot be determined without stakeholder resolution","Whether approximate location (e.g., zone-based, ETA-only) would satisfy Customer Success","Whether any form of location sharing would satisfy Operations' courier retention concern","Industry benchmarks on courier attrition vs. tracking granularity"],"assumptions":["Both teams are accurately representing their constraints — couriers are actually leaving, and customers actually want this","There is no existing executive directive that already resolves this conflict","The platform has the technical capability to offer variable granularity (exact GPS, zone-based, ETA-only)"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates visible to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires courier location to never be shown to customers. These are mutually exclusive, non-negotiable requirements from peer-level teams. Neither team has the authority to override the other.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers","Customer Success team","Operations team","Executive leadership"],"options":["A: Show exact GPS (satisfies Customer Success, violates Operations requirement, risks courier attrition)","B: Show no location (satisfies Operations, violates Customer Success requirement, risks customer churn)","C: Compromise — show approximate zone or ETA-based tracking instead of exact GPS (may partially satisfy both, requires both teams to soften their 'non-negotiable' stance)","D: Make it courier-opt-in — couriers choose whether to share location (respects courier autonomy but creates inconsistent customer experience)"],"recommendation":"Escalate to executive leadership for arbitration. Recommend Option C (approximate/zone-based tracking) as the starting point for discussion — it addresses the customer transparency need without enabling precise surveillance. However, this is a business strategy decision, not a domain analysis decision. Do not implement anything until this is resolved."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot implement exact GPS visibility as specified by Customer Success","business_rule_broken":"BR-3 — Courier safety and retention constraint. Showing precise real-time coordinates enables surveillance behavior that Operations has documented as causing driver attrition."},{"violation":"Cannot implement zero customer visibility as specified by Operations","business_rule_broken":"BR-4 — Customer transparency requirement. Providing no delivery progress information undermines the core value proposition of the tracking feature and increases support load."}]}
